name: Indonesian (Bahasa Indonesia)
notes: Indonesian (Bahasa Indonesia). Austronesian language (Malayo-Polynesian); official language of Indonesia. Based on Riau Malay; standardized from the 1920s. ~45 million L1 speakers; ~200 million L2. KEY FEATURES: (1) WORD-FINAL K: <k> in word-final position = [ʔ] (glottal stop), not [k]. This is the most critical positional rule. (2) E: two phonemes — <e> = [e] (close-mid, in open syllables) or [ə] (schwa, in closed syllables); contrast is partly predictable but not entirely. (3) WORD-FINAL H: may be silent or realized as [h] depending on word origin and register. (4) NG: [ŋ] word-initially possible (ngilu, nganga). (5) C: always [tʃ], never [k] or [s]. (6) KH: [x] from Arabic loans (khusus, akhir). (7) SY: [ʃ] from Arabic/Dutch loans (syarat, syukur). (8) DIPHTHONGS: ai/au/oi are true diphthongs in citation forms. (9) NASALIZATION: nasal prefixes meN-, peN- assimilate to following consonant (meng-, men-, mem-, meny-).

sources.author: Sneddon, J.N.
sources.title: The Indonesian Language: Its History and Role in Modern Society

sources.author: Lapoliwa, H.
sources.title: A Generative Approach to the Phonology of Bahasa Indonesia
sources.notes: Formal phonological analysis; key source for positional rules
